data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Leaps to 4-Month High as Trump Dials Down Stance on Iran
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield is up by 6.7 bps to a four-month high of 4.227% as investors digest easing geopolitical tensions and President Trump's nominee to replace Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I greatly respect the fact that all scheduled hangings, which were to take place yesterday (Over 800 of them), have been cancelled by the leadership of Iran," Trump said in a Truth Social post on Friday. Treasuries are viewed as a safe-haven asset, with easing geopolitical risks reducing demand for notes and bonds and pushing yields higher. The yield on Treasuries moves in the opposite direction of their prices.

Why is the 10-Year Treasury Yield Up Today?

Trump also noted that he would like White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett to remain in his current position. Following the comment, the odds of Trump nominating Hassett as Fed Chair dived to 17% from 31%, while former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh's odds rose to 61% from 34%.

Warsh is viewed by some economists as less likely to cut rates than Hassett. In other words, the 10-year Treasury yield rose as investors priced in a more hawkish Fed outlook under a possible Warsh nomination.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Forecast: Bear Case vs. Bull Case Scenario for 2026
data.description:
American chip giant Nvidia's stock (NVDA) continues to dominate headlines as the powerhouse behind the AI revolution, with its data center GPUs (graphics processing units) fueling record growth. After a robust 40% growth in 2025, investors face a pivotal question: will Nvidia sustain its momentum amid competition and macroeconomic headwinds, or surge to new heights on robust AI adoption? This article pits the bear case, steady but moderated gains, against the bull case of breakthrough innovations and market share expansion.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On TipRanks, the lowest price target of $200 suggests 6.9% upside potential over the next twelve months. Meanwhile, the highest price target of $352 implies 88.2% upside potential. Here's a closer look at both analysts' views.

NVDA Bear Case – $200 Price Target

Phillip Securities' four-star analyst Yik Ban Chong has a Buy rating on NVDA with a price target of $200. Following Nvidia's Q3 FY26 results, Chong raised his full year fiscal 2026 revenue forecast by 4% and profit by 10%. He attributed the faster ramp-up of the Blackwell GB300 chips, which would also boost gross margins by 0.5 percentage points.

While Chong remains concerned about the circular nature of AI deals with OpenAI ($100 billion) and Anthropic ($10 billion), he believes they represent less than 10% of Nvidia's $380 billion revenue visibility through the end of 2026.

He sees strong, persistent organic demand from hyperscalers' increased capex and sovereign deals in South Korea, Europe, and the UAE. Chong noted that NVDA traded at a forward P/E of 44.8x, below its historical 52.2x average.

NVDA Bull Case – $352 Price Target

Evercore ISI's five-star analyst Mark Lipacis also rates NVDA a Buy, with a $352 price target. He praised Nvidia's strong Q3 FY26 earnings, which showed faster revenue growth and more product availability. He also pointed to better supply of Blackwell-based GB200 and GB300 AI platforms, with Blackwell compute revenue jumping 48% quarter-over-quarter to add over $13 billion.

Nvidia reported $500 billion in data-center orders for Blackwell and advanced Rubin platforms through 2025-26. Despite tight supply concerns, Lipacis highlighted Nvidia's 32% higher Q3 inventory and 63% jump in supply commitments to handle these orders.

He views the projected $493 billion revenue for 2025-26 as conservative amid strong trends, signaling big stock upside if demand holds. His $352 price target is based on a 35x multiple on 2030 base-case EPS of $14.74, discounted back to 2026.

Is Nvidia a Good Buy Right Now?

Wall Street remains highly optimistic about Nvidia's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, NVDA commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $263.44 implies 40.8% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: UAA, MARA, HIMS: The 10 Most Shorted Stocks in the Market
data.description:
It's a new year and the stock market is off to a strong start. But that doesn't mean there aren't bears lurking on Wall Street.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The S&P 500 index is off to a decent start in 2026, rising more than 1% through the first two weeks of January. Ryan Detrick, Chief Market Strategist at wealth advisory firm Carson Group, points out that when the S&P 500 is up 1% or more after the first five trading days, then the full year is positive 87% of the time, with an average gain of 16%.

However, despite the positive start to the year, there are still plenty of stocks that investors and traders are shorting, meaning that they're betting on the share price declining. And the 10 most shorted stocks that have attracted bearish bets on Wall Street include the following names…

Wall Street's 10 Most Shorted Stocks

  1. Under Armour (UAA) – 34.96% short interest
  2. Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX) – 32.23%
  3. CleanSpark (CLSK) – 31.55%
  4. Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) – 30.88%
  5. Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) – 30.60%
  6. SoundHound AI (SOUN) – 28.59%
  7. MARA Holdings (MARA) – 27.45%
  8. Upstart Holdings (UPST) – 27.42%
  9. Applied Digital (APLD) – 27.27%
  10. Kohl's (KSS) – 25.86%

The most heavily shorted stocks range from struggling retailers such as Under Armour and Kohl's, to cryptocurrency mining firm MARA Holdings and telehealth concern Hims & Hers Health. All of the stocks on the list have experienced volatility in recent months.

As for the least shorted stocks on Wall Street currently, they are e-commerce giant Amazon (AMZN) and discount retailer Walmart (WMT). Less than 1% of AMZN and WMT stock is currently sold short, market data shows.

Is UAA Stock a Buy?

The stock of Under Armour has a consensus Hold rating among 18 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on four Buy, 11 Hold, and three Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average UAA price target of $6.00 implies 3.81% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 'Ryanair CEO Is an Utter Idiot,' Says Elon Musk in Latest Public Feud
data.description:
Elon Musk escalated a public feud on Friday by calling Ryanair (RYAAY) CEO Michael O'Leary an "utter idiot" and saying that he should be fired. The clash began earlier in the week after O'Leary ruled out installing Musk's Starlink internet service on Ryanair's aircraft. According to O'Leary, adding Starlink antennas would increase drag, raise fuel costs, and cost the airline as much as $250 million per year. As a result, he said Ryanair had no interest in adopting the technology.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Musk quickly pushed back on his social media platform X by arguing that O'Leary was "misinformed" and that the airline did not properly understand how to measure the fuel impact of Starlink equipment. However, the dispute became worse when O'Leary later spoke to Irish radio station Newstalk. During the interview, he said that Musk knew "zero" about aviation and aerodynamic drag. In addition, he openly called the billionaire an "idiot" and criticized X as a "cesspit." Unsurprisingly, the exchange did not end there.

Musk, whose space company SpaceX operates Starlink, responded again on Friday by posting, "Ryanair CEO is an utter idiot. Fire him." When one user suggested Musk should simply buy Ryanair and remove O'Leary himself, Musk replied, "Good idea." The argument comes as airlines become a growing market for Starlink, which uses thousands of low-Earth-orbit satellites to deliver faster in-flight WiFi. In fact, more than two dozen airlines are already rolling out the service, though analysts say its premium nature makes it more attractive to long-haul and full-service carriers rather than budget airlines like Ryanair.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and eight Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $394.12 per share implies 10% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Waver as Trump Considers New Tariffs ahead of 3-Day Market Closure
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed slightly lower on Friday following several announcements from President Trump. The stock market will be closed on Monday, January 19 in honor of Martin Luther King Jr. Day.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

President Trump's interest in acquiring Greenland has risen substantially since the U.S. successfully captured Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro. Now, he's considering imposing a tariff on countries that don't support that goal. "I may put on a tariff on countries if they don't go along with Greenland because we need Greenland for national security," Trump said at a healthcare roundtable event without mentioning a timeline or a specific rate.

Furthermore, Trump stated that he prefers White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett, a top contender to replace Fed Chair Jerome Powell, to remain in his current role. Following his comment, Hassett's odds of being nominated plunged to 17% from 31% on the prediction platform Polymarket. On the other hand, former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh's odds climbed to 59% from 34%. Trump has communicated to allies that he appreciates Hassett's tendency to support his policies on television, according to The Wall Street Journal.

Earlier in the day, Hassett downplayed the Department of Justice's investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's costly renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. "The bottom line ‌is, I expect, you know, Jay is a good man – I expect that there's nothing to see here, that the cost overruns are related to things like asbestos, as he says," Hassett told Fox News.

Powell has received an outpouring of support from economic leaders around the world after he disclosed the investigation against him, framing it as a pretext for the Fed setting monetary policy independently instead of favoring Trump's preferences for lower rates.

Elsewhere, Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman warned of a "sharp correction in equity prices" if AI investments result in lackluster returns. At the same time, she also cited AI as a contributor to rising productivity, which factored into her willingness to cut rates last year. Bowman added that she supports additional interest rate cuts in order to support a fragile labor market. The Fed is set to meet for its first Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting of the year on January 28.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.06% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 0.07%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Trump Weighs Tariff on Countries that Oppose U.S. Acquisition of Greenland
data.description:
President Trump is considering imposing a tariff against countries that oppose an acquisition of Greenland by the U.S.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I may put on a tariff on countries if they don't go along with Greenland because we need Greenland for national security," Trump said at a healthcare roundtable event on Friday. He didn't immediately specify a timetable or what the rate would be.

Will the U.S. Acquire Greenland?

Trump has spoken considerably about annexing Greenland since the U.S. captured Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro earlier this month. He previously characterized the autonomous territory as a strategic asset given its location and potential role in monitoring international rivals, such as China and Russia.

Earlier today, a group of bipartisan lawmakers met with Greenlandic and Danish leaders to discuss U.S.-Greenland relations. Delaware Senator Chris Coons noted that the meeting resulted in a "constructive and hopeful dialogue" and emphasized that it is up to the people of Greenland to decide on their future.

data.title: Micron (MU) Breaks Ground on $100 Billion Semiconductor Manufacturing Plant
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) has broken ground on a new $100 billion semiconductor manufacturing facility located in upstate New York.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Executives at Micron are calling the memory chip manufacturing complex situated in Onondaga County near Syracuse the "largest semiconductor facility in the United States." Once the facility is completed and fully operational in 2030, it is expected to generate 50,000 jobs in New York state.

"As the largest private investment in New York state history, Micron's Central New York project will be home to the most advanced memory manufacturing in the world and will help meet the growing demands of AI systems and devices that are central to the modern economy," said Micron in a statement.

Micron's Big Investment

In addition to the $100 billion being spent to construct the new manufacturing plant, Micron says it will also invest $250 million in workforce development, education, transportation, and housing through a community investment program.

Over the past year, Micron has announced plans to expand its U.S. investments to $150 billion, composed of domestic manufacturing and research and development (R&D). The microchip maker has also pledged to create 90,000 direct and indirect jobs in the U.S. MU stock has risen more than 200% over the last year.

Is MU Stock a Buy?

Micron's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 26 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 25 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average MU price target of $363.77 implies 1.43% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Netflix Stock (NASDAQ:NFLX) Notches Up as Netflix Promises Warner Theatrical Window
data.description:
One of the biggest objections to streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) taking over Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) is that Netflix is on record, several times, as saying the movie theater is obsolete. Thus, any future releases from Warner will likely end up going straight to Netflix and, probably, creating a self-fulfilling prophecy. But Netflix came out on record against such a play, promising a 45-day window for Warner releases. This gave Netflix shares just a little extra life, a fractional boost going into the closing minutes of Friday's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Netflix co-CEO Ted Sarandos recently noted that Warner titles would be hitting theaters, and declared the 45-day theatrical window would be respected. This by itself only meant so much, as we had heard previously that Netflix would be honoring contractual obligations when it came to theatrical releases. Netflix was likely doing this more to avoid lawsuits than it was to protect a platform it regards as obsolete.

But Sarandos raised a point that many others—us included—have raised before. The box office is a massive revenue source. Just look at the sales grosses on any typical Monday and see what theaters pulled in over a weekend. Sarandos made it especially clear: "I want to win opening weekend. I want to win box office." Indeed, Sarandos also discovered that theatrical business numbers were "…more positive than we had seen and we had modeled for ourselves." The theater is still a profitable business, and Netflix wants in on that.

Perfect Example, Really

In a display of what might be considered good faith, or a modeling of the plan as a whole, reports note that the live-action Legend of Zelda movie will be making its streaming debut on Netflix. What is more, it will remain there, exclusively. It will go to theaters, and it will also get a "home entertainment release period." But once those are up, Zelda will be hitting Netflix and staying there for some time to come.

Either way, there will still be quite a wait before this hits. The movie will not hit theaters until May 7, 2027, and will still have to go through the physical and digital purchase runs before hitting the streamer. Still, if anyone wanted proof that Netflix was planning to respect theatrical releases, this might do the job.

Is Netflix Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on NFLX stock based on 26 Buys, nine Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 37.03% rally in its share price over the past year, the average NFLX price target of $127.23 per share implies 44.59% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Alphabet (GOOGL) Introduces Parental Controls for YouTube
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL) is introducing parental controls for its popular video platform YouTube.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Going forward, Alphabet will let parents limit the amount of time their children and teens can spend scrolling through its short-form video feature called "Shorts," or block those videos altogether. The new parental controls are part of efforts to protect young users and respond to growing criticism from families and children advocates about the impacts of social media and videos on mental health.

Alphabet, which is also the parent company of the Google search engine, has acknowledged that endlessly scrolling through short videos can make social media platforms addictive, especially for children and teenagers. Parents supervising their teen's account can now set a time limit on Shorts, ranging from two hours to zero minutes.

Bedtime Reminders on YouTube

In addition to limiting the time spent on Shorts or blocking the short-form videos entirely, parents can also set bedtime and "take a break" reminders for their kids to help limit their time on the platform. Alphabet said that YouTube is also adding a new sign-up process to make it easier for parents to create supervised accounts for their kids and teens.

And, YouTube is updating its guidelines for the types of content that will be recommended and accessible to teenagers. It will prioritize videos centered around "curiosity and inspiration," "building life skills and experiences" and "information that supports wellbeing." Teens will be blocked from repeatedly viewing videos with content deemed to be dangerous or that could send them down rabbit holes.

Is GOOGL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Alphabet has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 34 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 27 Buy and seven Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average GOOGL price target of $342.39 implies 3.80% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Slips as Heckler Ford Worker Walks Away With Huge Donations
data.description:
Legacy automaker Ford (F) recently played host to President Trump. For the most part, the event went smoothly, until one worker decided to register his distaste for Trump as loudly as he could generate. The worker was subsequently suspended, reports note, but some took it upon themselves to get the poor guy some cash. GoFundMe accounts were set up, and raised a huge amount for the worker, TJ Sabula. Ford shares, meanwhile, dropped modestly as a Ford worker who heckled a president is now pretty much set for life.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Two accounts were established to raise cash for Sabula, reports noted, both through GoFundMe. The first one pulled in over $480,000 in donations by itself. A second account brought in $330,000. Thus, combined, a sum over $800,000 was raised for the worker. Over 21,000 separate donations were registered.

Sabula's cry of "Pedophile protector," aimed at Trump, was regarded as a "patriotic" act. Meanwhile, Trump's response of an upraised middle finger was regarded as "vulgar." The exact nature of either of these acts is, of course, subject to interpretation. But if people start figuring that heckling a visitor on the job, especially if that visitor is of sufficient standing, could result in a near-million-dollar-payday, well, that could have substantial ramifications. And it certainly does not help Ford look much better.

Car Moms Love Fords

Meanwhile, Ford managed to land a significant accolade with a major target market: families. Specifically, Ford took home top honors as the 2026 Car Mom Car of the Year with its 2025 Ford Expedition.

The Car Mom in question is Kelly Stumpe, mother of four and a Certified Child Passenger Safety Technician. So when it comes to finding cars that are as family-safe as they are family-friendly, the Car Mom is a pretty good direction to look for advice. Stumpe gave the Ford Expedition the nod thanks to its use of car seats—the seats can actually modify to fit a growing family's needs—specialized and highly useful third-row seating, and more.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on three Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 35.66% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.77 per share implies 0.92% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
